title: Han van der Maas
gist: A thin SFI faculty profile of Han van der Maas, External Professor and chair of Psychological Methods at the University of Amsterdam, whose work formalizes and tests psychological theories of cognition, development, and intelligence — including phase transitions in cognitive development — and who founded the adaptive child-monitoring ed-tech spin-off Oefenweb (now part of Prowise).

insights.claim: Han van der Maas's research formalizes and tests psychological theories in cognition, expertise, development, attitudes, and intelligence, including methods for analyzing phase transitions in cognitive development.
insights.speaker: Santa Fe Institute
insights.so_what: Identifies a complexity-science researcher applying phase-transition and formal-modeling methods to human cognition and intelligence.
insights.quote: His general research theme is the formalization and testing of psychological theories in areas such as cognition, expertise, development, attitudes and intelligence.

insights.claim: Van der Maas founded Oefenweb.nl in 2009, a University of Amsterdam spin-off selling a game-based adaptive child-monitoring system, now part of Prowise.com.
insights.speaker: Santa Fe Institute
insights.so_what: An academic-to-commercial path in adaptive learning tech; Prowise's absorption of Oefenweb marks consolidation in the European ed-tech space.
insights.quote: selling an unique  game-based web-based adaptive child monitoring system.  Oefenweb is now part of Prowise.com.
